Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Min and max of a single-element array are equal to that element.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SingleMinMax
  (defun fmin (a n)
    (def m (arr.get a 0))
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do (if (< (arr.get a i) m) (set m (arr.get a i)) 0) (set i (+ i 1))))
    m)
  (defun fmax (a n)
    (def m (arr.get a 0))
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do (if (> (arr.get a i) m) (set m (arr.get a i)) 0) (set i (+ i 1))))
    m)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 1))
    (arr.set a 0 42)
    (assert-eq (fmin a 1) 42)
    (assert-eq (fmax a 1) 42))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Solar: monthly_savings * 12 = annual.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SolarS
  (defun min2 (a b)
    (if (< a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun monthly_generated (panels watt sun days)
    (/ (* panels (* watt (* sun days))) 1000))
  (defun monthly_savings (used generated rate)
    (* (min2 used generated) rate))
  (defun annual_savings (panels watt sun days used rate)
    (def gen (monthly_generated panels watt sun days))
    (* (monthly_savings used gen rate) 12))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun yearly (panels watt sun days used rate)
    (round2 (annual_savings panels watt sun days used rate)))
  (test small (near? (yearly 5 250 5 30 800 0.15) 337.5 0.05))
  (test full (near? (yearly 10 300 6 30 540 0.20) 1296.0 0.05)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Map score to grade. Boundaries are inclusive at the lower edge: 90→A, 80→B, 70→C, 60→D, else F.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Grade
  (defun grade (s)
    (if (>= s 90.0) (return "A"))
    (if (>= s 80.0) (return "B"))
    (if (>= s 70.0) (return "C"))
    (if (>= s 60.0) (return "D"))
    "F")
  (test exact_a (eq? (grade 90.0) "A"))
  (test exact_b (eq? (grade 80.0) "B"))
  (test exact_c (eq? (grade 70.0) "C"))
  (test exact_d (eq? (grade 60.0) "D"))
  (test below_d (eq? (grade 59.99) "F"))
  (test high (eq? (grade 100.0) "A")))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Largest prime factor of n (iterative trial division).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module LargestPF
  (defun lpf (n)
    (def x n)
    (def d 2)
    (def best 1)
    (while (> x 1)
      (do
        (if (= (math.mod x d) 0)
          (do (set best d) (set x (/ x d)))
          (set d (+ d 1)))))
    best)
  (test n12 (eq? (lpf 12) 3))
  (test n13 (eq? (lpf 13) 13))
  (test n100 (eq? (lpf 100) 5)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Rental cost: every 7 days costs 6×daily, remainder at daily; +insurance per actual day; if loyalty=1 → 10% off; +tax_pct%. Round to 2 decimals.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Rental
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))
  (defun base_days (days daily)
    (def weeks (math.floor (/ days 7.0)))
    (def rem (- days (* weeks 7.0)))
    (+ (* weeks (* 6.0 daily)) (* rem daily)))
  (defun rental_cost (days daily insurance loyalty tax_pct)
    (def base (base_days days daily))
    (def with_ins (+ base (* days insurance)))
    (def discounted (if (= loyalty 1) (* with_ins 0.9) with_ins))
    (def taxed (* discounted (+ 1.0 (/ tax_pct 100.0))))
    (round2 taxed))
  (test one_day (near? (rental_cost 1.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 60.0 0.01))
  (test week (near? (rental_cost 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 370.0 0.01))
  (test eight_days (near? (rental_cost 8.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 430.0 0.01))
  (test loyalty (near? (rental_cost 7.0 50.0 10.0 1 0.0) 333.0 0.01))
  (test with_tax (near? (rental_cost 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 10.0) 407.0 0.01))
  (test loyalty_tax (near? (rental_cost 7.0 50.0 10.0 1 10.0) 366.3 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

$30 base, +$0.10/min for first 100 over, +$0.05/min thereafter.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module PhoneTiered
  (defun min2 (a b) (if (< a b) (return a)) b)
  (defun max2 (a b) (if (> a b) (return a)) b)
  (defun bill (mins included)
    (def base 30.0)
    (def over (max2 0 (- mins included)))
    (def tier1 (* (min2 over 100) 0.10))
    (def tier2 (* (max2 0 (- over 100)) 0.05))
    (+ base (+ tier1 tier2)))
  (test under (near? (bill 500 1000) 30.0 0.01))
  (test slight_over (near? (bill 1050 1000) 35.0 0.01))
  (test heavy_over (near? (bill 1300 1000) 50.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum of squares of the decimal digits of n (one Happy-number step).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HappyStep
  (defun step (n)
    (def s 0)
    (def x n)
    (while (> x 0)
      (do
        (def d (math.mod x 10))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set x (/ (- x d) 10))))
    s)
  (test n19 (eq? (step 19) 82))
  (test n7 (eq? (step 7) 49))
  (test n1 (eq? (step 1) 1)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Count unique values in sorted array.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Unique
  (defun count_unique (xs n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def c 1)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (if (= (arr_get xs i) (arr_get xs (- i 1))) 0 (set c (+ c 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    c)
  (test all_same
    (def a (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set a 0 5) (arr_set a 1 5) (arr_set a 2 5) (arr_set a 3 5)
    (eq? (count_unique a 4) 1))
  (test all_diff
    (def a (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 2) (arr_set a 2 3) (arr_set a 3 4)
    (eq? (count_unique a 4) 4))
  (test mixed
    (def a (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 1) (arr_set a 2 2) (arr_set a 3 3) (arr_set a 4 3)
    (eq? (count_unique a 5) 3)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Clip array values to [0, 10] in-place, then sum.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ClipSum
  (defun clip (a n lo hi)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (if (< (arr.get a i) lo) (arr.set a i lo) 0)
        (if (> (arr.get a i) hi) (arr.set a i hi) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    a)
  (defun sumv (a n)
    (def s 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n) (do (set s (+ s (arr.get a i))) (set i (+ i 1))))
    s)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 -5) (arr.set a 1 3) (arr.set a 2 15) (arr.set a 3 8) (arr.set a 4 12)
    (clip a 5 0 10)
    (assert-eq (sumv a 5) 31))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 1 if string is a palindrome, 0 otherwise.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module IsPalindrome
  (defun is_palindrome (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (def i 0)
    (def j (- n 1))
    (while (< i j)
      (if (= (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) (str_substring s j 1)) false)
        (return 0))
      (set i (+ i 1))
      (set j (- j 1)))
    1)
  (test empty (eq? (is_palindrome "") 1))
  (test single (eq? (is_palindrome "a") 1))
  (test yes (eq? (is_palindrome "racecar") 1))
  (test no (eq? (is_palindrome "hello") 0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum of squared differences between consecutive elements.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SqDiffs
  (defun ssd (a n)
    (def s 0)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def d (- (arr.get a i) (arr.get a (- i 1))))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    s)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 3) (arr.set a 2 2) (arr.set a 3 6)
    (assert-eq (ssd a 4) 21))))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Stream: base + family extension + hi-fi addon - student discount.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module MusicStream
  (defun base_cost () 10)
  (defun family_addon (members)
    (if (<= members 1) (return 0))
    (* (- members 1) 3))
  (defun hifi_addon (has_hifi)
    (if (= has_hifi 1) 5 0))
  (defun student_discount (cost is_student)
    (if (= is_student 1) (return (* cost 0.5)))
    cost)
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun monthly (members has_hifi is_student)
    (def total (+ (base_cost) (+ (family_addon members) (hifi_addon has_hifi))))
    (round2 (student_discount total is_student)))
  (test single (near? (monthly 1 0 0) 10.0 0.01))
  (test family (near? (monthly 4 0 0) 19.0 0.01))
  (test student (near? (monthly 1 0 1) 5.0 0.01))
  (test family_hifi (near? (monthly 4 1 0) 24.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Length of the longest run of equal consecutive values.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module LongestRun
  (defun lr (a n)
    (def best 1)
    (def cur 1)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (if (= (arr.get a i) (arr.get a (- i 1)))
          (do (set cur (+ cur 1)) (if (> cur best) (set best cur) 0))
          (set cur 1))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    best)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 7))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 2) (arr.set a 3 2) (arr.set a 4 3) (arr.set a 5 3) (arr.set a 6 4)
    (assert-eq (lr a 7) 3))))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Read file content; compute integer average word length (sum of lengths / count); 0 if empty.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module AvgWordLen
  (extern defun file_read (p) @capability "file.read")
  (defun avg_wl (p)
    (def c (file_read p))
    (if (str_eq c "") (return 0))
    (def parts : (Array Str) (str_split c " "))
    (def n (arr.length parts))
    (def total 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (set total (+ total (str_length (arr.get parts i))))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (math.floor (/ total n)))
  (test t (mocks (file.read "/x" "the quick fox")) (eq? (avg_wl "/x") 3))
  (test single (mocks (file.read "/x" "hello")) (eq? (avg_wl "/x") 5))
  (test empty (mocks (file.read "/x" "")) (eq? (avg_wl "/x") 0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Binary search; return index or -1.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module BinSearch
  (defun bsearch (a n target)
    (def lo 0) (def hi (- n 1)) (def r -1) (def done 0)
    (while (= done 0)
      (do
        (if (> lo hi) (set done 1)
          (do
            (def mid (math.floor (/ (+ lo hi) 2)))
            (def v (arr.get a mid))
            (if (= v target) (do (set r mid) (set done 1))
              (if (< v target) (set lo (+ mid 1)) (set hi (- mid 1))))))))
    r)

Compute (base^exp) mod m using repeated squaring.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ModExp
  (defun modexp (base exp m)
    (def r 1)
    (def b (math.mod base m))
    (def e exp)
    (while (> e 0)
      (do
        (if (= (math.mod e 2) 1) (set r (math.mod (* r b) m)) 0)
        (set b (math.mod (* b b) m))
        (set e (/ (- e (math.mod e 2)) 2))))
    r)
  (test small (eq? (modexp 2 10 1000) 24))
  (test fermat (eq? (modexp 3 5 7) 5))
  (test one (eq? (modexp 7 0 11) 1)))

    (def n (str_length s))
    (def c 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def in_word 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def ch (str_substring s i 1))
        (if (= (is_ws ch) 1)
          (set in_word 0)
          (do

            (set in_word 1)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    c)

  (test two (eq? (word_count "hello world") 2))

(module BinSearch
  (defun bsearch (xs n target)
    (def lo 0)
    (def hi (- n 1))
    (while (<= lo hi)
      (def mid (math_floor (/ (+ lo hi) 2)))
      (def m (arr_get xs mid))
      (if (= m target) (return mid))
      (if (< m target) (set lo (+ mid 1)) (set hi (- mid 1))))
    -1)
  (test found
    (def a (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 3) (arr_set a 2 5) (arr_set a 3 7) (arr_set a 4 9)
    (eq? (bsearch a 5 5) 2))
  (test not_found
    (def a (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 2) (arr_set a 2 3)
    (eq? (bsearch a 3 99) -1))
  (test first
    (def a (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 2) (arr_set a 2 3)
    (eq? (bsearch a 3 1) 0)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Insertion sort in place; verify ascending result.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module InsertionSort
  (defun isort (a n)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def key (arr.get a i))
        (def j (- i 1))
        (def keep_going 1)
        (while (= keep_going 1)
          (do
            (if (< j 0) (set keep_going 0)
              (if (<= (arr.get a j) key) (set keep_going 0)
                (do
                  (arr.set a (+ j 1) (arr.get a j))
                  (set j (- j 1)))))))
        (arr.set a (+ j 1) key)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    a)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 4) (arr.set a 1 1) (arr.set a 2 3) (arr.set a 3 5) (arr.set a 4 2)
    (isort a 5)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 0) 1)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 1) 2)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 4) 5))))

    (def out : (Array Num) (arr.new n))
    (def k 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (if (> (arr.get a i) 0) (do (arr.set out k (arr.get a i)) (set k (+ k 1))) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    out)
  (defun count_pos (a n)
    (def c 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (if (> (arr.get a i) 0) (set c (+ c 1)) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    c)
  (defun bsort (a m)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i (- m 1))
      (do
        (def j 0)
        (while (< j (- m (+ i 1)))
          (do
            (def x (arr.get a j))
            (def y (arr.get a (+ j 1)))
            (if (> x y)
              (do (arr.set a j y) (arr.set a (+ j 1) x))
              0)
            (set j (+ j 1))))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    a)
  (defun smallest_pos (a n)
    (def filt : (Array Num) (keep_pos a n))
    (def m (count_pos a n))
    (bsort filt m)
    (arr.get filt 0))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 1 if parentheses in s are balanced (other chars ignored), else 0. Empty string is balanced.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Balanced
  (defun is_balanced (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (def depth 0)
    (def fail 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def ch (str_substring s i 1))
        (if (= ch "(") (set depth (+ depth 1)) 0)
        (if (= ch ")")
          (do
            (if (= depth 0) (set fail 1) 0)
            (if (> depth 0) (set depth (- depth 1)) 0))
          0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (if (= fail 1) (return 0))
    (if (= depth 0) 1 0))
  (test empty (eq? (is_balanced "") 1))
  (test pair (eq? (is_balanced "()") 1))
  (test nested (eq? (is_balanced "(())") 1))
  (test siblings (eq? (is_balanced "(()())") 1))
  (test ignore (eq? (is_balanced "(a(b)c)") 1))
  (test reversed (eq? (is_balanced ")(") 0))
  (test unclosed (eq? (is_balanced "((") 0))
  (test trailing (eq? (is_balanced "())") 0))
  (test bare_text (eq? (is_balanced "abc") 1)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Copy only the first line (up to first '\n') from src to dst; return its length.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module CopyFirstLine
  (extern defun file_read ((path : Str)) : Str @capability "file.read")
  (extern defun file_write ((path : Str) (body : Str)) : Num @capability "file.write")
  (defun first_line (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (def i 0)
    (def done 0)
    (while (= done 0)
      (do
        (if (>= i n) (set done 1)
          (if (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) "\n") (set done 1)
            (set i (+ i 1))))))
    (str_substring s 0 i))
  (defun copy_fl (src dst)
    (def line (first_line (file_read src)))
    (file_write dst line)
    (str_length line))
  (test multi (mocks (file.read "/s" "abc\ndef") (file.write "/d" 1)) (assert-eq (copy_fl "/s" "/d") 3))
  (test single (mocks (file.read "/s" "hello") (file.write "/d" 1)) (assert-eq (copy_fl "/s" "/d") 5))
  (test empty (mocks (file.read "/s" "") (file.write "/d" 1)) (assert-eq (copy_fl "/s" "/d") 0)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Compute mean then population variance over a small array.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module StatsPipe
  (defun mean_n (a n)
    (def s 0.0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n) (do (set s (+ s (arr.get a i))) (set i (+ i 1))))
    (/ s n))
  (defun var_n (a n)
    (def mu (mean_n a n))
    (def s 0.0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def d (- (arr.get a i) mu))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (/ s n))
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 2.0) (arr.set a 1 4.0) (arr.set a 2 4.0) (arr.set a 3 4.0)
    (assert-near (mean_n a 4) 3.5 0.001)
    (assert-near (var_n a 4) 0.75 0.001))))

Return 1 if parens balanced and never negative depth, 0 otherwise.

(module BalancedParens
  (defun balanced (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (def i 0)
    (def d 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def c (str_substring s i 1))
      (if (str_eq c "(") (set d (+ d 1)))
      (if (str_eq c ")") (set d (- d 1)))
      (if (< d 0) (return 0))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    (if (= d 0) 1 0))
  (test empty (eq? (balanced "") 1))
  (test simple (eq? (balanced "()") 1))
  (test nested (eq? (balanced "((()))") 1))

  (defun lbl (x)

(module LPF

        (set best f)
        (set x (/ x f)))
      (set f (+ f 1)))
    (if (> x 1) (set best x))
    best)

    (if (= found 0) n d))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Generalized Armstrong check: sum of digits^k == n where k is number of digits.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ArmGen
  (defun digit_count (n)
    (def x n)
    (def k 0)
    (if (= n 0) (return 1))
    (while (> x 0)
      (do
        (set k (+ k 1))
        (set x (/ (- x (math.mod x 10)) 10))))
    k)
  (defun pow_int (b e)
    (def r 1)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i e) (do (set r (* r b)) (set i (+ i 1))))
    r)
  (defun digit_pow_sum (n k)
    (def x n)
    (def s 0)
    (while (> x 0)
      (do
        (def d (math.mod x 10))
        (set s (+ s (pow_int d k)))
        (set x (/ (- x d) 10))))
    s)
  (defun is_arm (n)
    (def k (digit_count n))
    (if (= n (digit_pow_sum n k)) 1 0))
  (test a153 (eq? (is_arm 153) 1))
  (test a370 (eq? (is_arm 370) 1))
  (test a9474 (eq? (is_arm 9474) 1))
  (test a100 (eq? (is_arm 100) 0)))
